name: Short Rib Mole
recipeIngredient: Beef or chicken stock, or water, enough to cover the ribs
recipeInstructions: Preheat the oven to 300°F. Season the ribs generously with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tablespoons of neutral oil in a dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the ribs on all sides, about 6 to 8 minutes per side. Transfer ribs to a plate.

Cook onion and carrot in the same pot until browned, about 5 minutes. Return ribs to the pot, add bay leaf, and cover with your choice of liquid (beef or chicken stock, or water). Bake, covered tightly, for about 3 hours or until very tender.
First, remove the seeds and veins from the chiles. Then, toast the chiles on your comal or sauté pan, about 20 seconds on each side. Once toasted, set aside.
Toast the tortilla until crisp and slightly charred, with some dark brown-black spots. Set aside.
Toast the peanuts, almonds, pumpkin seeds, sesame seeds, cloves, and peppercorns until golden. Remove from comal and set aside.
Toast the onion and garlic until fragrant and lightly charred. Set aside.
Lastly, cook the tomato until charred patches appear, then remove from heat.

Blend all the charred ingredients (chiles, tortilla, peanuts, almonds, pumpkin seeds, sesame seeds, cloves, peppercorns, onion, garlic, and tomato) with raisins, cookies, cinnamon, along with one cup of rib cooking liquid and add more as needed until you reach a smooth, uniform, pourable consistency.
Heat a large clean pot or very deep pan to medium-low heat and add 2 tablespoons of neutral oil. Then pour the mole into the pot, simmer for 10 minutes.

Break the chocolate into pieces and add to the pan with a good pinch of salt. Mix well and continue to cook for another 10 minutes, stirring every minute. The mole will thicken as it simmers. You can add more broth or water to help loosen the mole.

Spoon half of the mole into a baking dish. Place ribs on top and spoon remaining mole over ribs. Broil or bake until sizzling and browned, about 2 to 4 minutes per side under the broiler or 8 to 12 minutes in the oven.
To serve, place the short rib on a freshly made tortilla and spoon some of the mole sauce on top. ¡Provecho!
author: masienda.com
